findings.evidence: Grep hits: `Regulator.lean:59` adds the `≤ 1` theorem, while `Regulator.lean:65` already has `rank_eq_zero_of_card_infinitePlace_eq_one`; Mathlib has `InfinitePlace.Basic.lean:59: instance [Nonempty (K →+* ℂ)] : Nonempty (InfinitePlace K)`, so for a number field the `≤ 1` hypothesis is just the equality case.
findings.fix: Do not add a separate named rank-zero lemma for `≤ 1`; at use sites convert `Fintype.card (InfinitePlace K) ≤ 1` to equality using nonemptiness/card positivity and call `rank_eq_zero_of_card_infinitePlace_eq_one`.
findings.issue: `rank_eq_zero_of_card_infinitePlace_le_one` duplicates the existing `rank_eq_zero_of_card_infinitePlace_eq_one` API up to the standard nonemptiness of `InfinitePlace K`.

summary: The new monotone infinite-place rank criterion is only a parallel wrapper around the existing single-infinite-place criterion, since number fields already have a nonempty finite type of infinite places.
